title: EXECUTION_PLAN.md Schema (REMEDIATE mode)
description: Schema for the YAML front-matter and structured-step body of EXECUTION_PLAN.md, the artifact REMEDIATE Phase 1 produces and Phase 2 consumes. v1 — coordinated with rootnode-repo-hygiene HYGIENE_REPORT.md format v1.

plan_id.description: Stable identifier for the plan. UUID or short kebab-case identifier. Used in CHANGELOG entries and rollback references.

generated_by.description: Skill identifier and version that produced the plan. Format: 'rootnode-cc-design v<version>'.

input_report.description: Path (relative to repo root) of the HYGIENE_REPORT.md the plan consumes. For CP-only invocation with pasted findings, use 'inline'.

addresses_findings.description: Array of finding identifiers from the input report that this plan addresses. Each must reference a finding present in the report. Finding IDs follow the F-{category}.{n} format owned by rootnode-repo-hygiene.

summary.description: Human-readable one-paragraph summary of what the plan does. Suitable for surfacing in chat between Phase 1 and acceptance.

estimated_diff_size_lines.description: Optional: estimated total line-count impact of the plan (sum of additions + deletions across all edit/create steps).

conflicts_requiring_user_decision.description: Conflicts that the default sequencing rules could not resolve. Phase 2 will halt at each marked step and require explicit user choice.

pre_flight_validation.description: Validations to run before any step. Phase 2 halts before step 1 if any pre-flight check fails.

steps.description: Ordered list of plan steps. Phase 2 walks them in order.

post_flight_validation.description: Validations to run after all steps complete. Phase 2 halts at the end if any post-flight check fails.

step_id.description: Unique identifier within the plan. Used in halt reports and dependency references.

title.description: Human-readable step title. Surfaces in chat output during Phase 2.

addresses_finding.description: Finding identifier(s) this step addresses. May reference multiple findings if a single step closes multiple findings. Bare format 'F-X.Y' or with optional inline canonical-reference label, e.g. 'F-1.1 (§2.1 bloated CLAUDE.md)'.

risk.description: Step risk tag for conditional approval. Determined at Phase 1 plan generation based on the structural fix's blast radius. 'high' = irreversible operations, large-scope changes, settings/permission modifications, schema migrations, hook additions to existing settings.json. 'medium' = structured edits to multiple files, new file creation in important paths, CLAUDE.md restructuring. 'low' = single-line fixes, comment-only updates, minor formatting changes, CHANGELOG appends. Conditional approval ("execute medium-and-low risk; halt on high-risk") filters Phase 2 by this tag.

action.description: Action type. See remediate-mode-execution.md §5 for semantics.

target.description: For file actions: path relative to repo root. For run actions: shell command (allowlist applies — see §5).

payload.description: Action-type-specific payload. Schema varies by action type (see oneOf below at top-level once Phase 2 is implemented; v1 spec is descriptive).

oneOf.description: Diff (unified format) for edit; full content for create; (none) for delete and run
Structured op for modify: {op, path, value}

validation.description: Post-step validations. See §6 for the validation grammar.

on_failure.description: What Phase 2 does on step validation failure. v1 only supports 'halt'.

requires_user_choice.description: Set true for steps in unresolved conflicts. Phase 2 halts and prompts the user before running these steps.

check.description: Validation check type. See remediate-mode-execution.md §6 for the grammar.

substring.description: Substring to check for (for file_contains / file_not_contains).

equals.description: Expected value (for json_path or command_output checks). Type varies by use.

cmd.description: Shell command to run (for command_* checks). Allowlist applies.

expect_lt.description: Expected output less than this integer (for command_succeeds where output is numeric).

expect_gt.description: Expected output greater than this integer (for command_succeeds where output is numeric).
